content: in account settings pane, highlight the desired account in left sidebar. Then, click the 'managed identities' button in right side. There, you can enter the same email info, but with a fancier signature (I do that). Then, when composing a message, you can click the FROM dropdown an select the fancier email.

content:
If it's a well-known email provider, Thunderbird may know the proper server settings based on your email address, and do a good basic job of setting it up for you. You will be prompted through this when you first install Thunderbird. After that, go to <Account Settings> and click the "New Account" button.

If this is a less well-know provider or the automatic process fails, gather the correct settings from their support web site. You will need to know:

for incoming: Protocol (IMAP, in this case) Hostname/server name Port Connection security (STARTTLS or SSL/TLS) Authentication method (usually Normal Password or OAuth2)

Same info needed for the outgoing (SMTP) server. 

Then start the New Account process, and after you enter your email address, you'll be presented with a "Configure manually" option where you can enter the server information you gathered.
creator: rednoise

content:
The last version of Thunderbird to support Windows 7 is 115.18.0 . It can be downloaded here:

https://www.thunderbird.net/en-US/thunderbird/all/

When you eventually are ready to move to Windows 11, I suggest that you install that same version there, then copy your Profile from the Win7 machine to the 11 machine. After you're comfortable that it's working the way you like, THEN upgrade Thunderbird.
